---
title: "ggpaintr Manual Test Workbook"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

This workbook is the canonical interactive manual test guide for the current `ggpaintr` workflow. It is intended to complement, not replace, the package-standard automated tests in `tests/testthat/`.

It aligns with:

-   `.claude/specs/testing-strategy.md`
-   `tests/manual/manual-checklist-ggpaintr.md`
-   `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd`

Current automated coverage for runtime feedback and the copy-rule system already includes:

-   malformed `expr` handling in `ptr_complete_expr_safe()`
-   invalid upload handling in `ptr_complete_expr_safe()`
-   plot-stage missing-object handling in `ptr_assemble_plot_safe()`
-   successful runtime assembly in `ptr_exec()`
-   copy-rule validation, fallback resolution, and alias handling
-   default and overridden copy for app-shell controls
-   custom Shiny integration helpers with custom top-level ids
-   custom placeholder registry parsing and runtime replacement
-   low-level runtime input discovery through `ptr_runtime_input_spec()`

See:

-   `tests/testthat/test-runtime-feedback.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-copy-rules.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-extensibility.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-placeholder-registry.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-runtime-input-spec.R`

## Coverage Map

This workbook is intended to cover the maintained `ggpaintr` surface end to end.
Use the sections below as the canonical manual sweep for:

-   core app flow, plot updates, generated code, and layer toggles
-   built-in placeholders: `var`, `text`, `num`, `expr`, and `upload`
-   runtime error feedback, recovery, and plot-stage failure behavior
-   default copy rules and targeted `ui_text` overrides
-   embedded `ptr_server_state()` / bind-helper integrations
-   custom plot rendering with `ptr_extract_plot()`
-   custom placeholder registration
-   companion edge-case follow-up in `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd`

## How To Use This Workbook

Use this file from an interactive RStudio session.

1.  Run the setup chunk first.
2.  Review the printed fixture paths.
3.  Run one app chunk at a time to launch `ptr_app()`.
4.  Close the current app before launching the next one.
5.  Use the expected-check lists below to record pass/fail behavior.

## Error Feedback Feature Test Guide

Use the scenarios below to test the dedicated inline error channel end to end.

| Scenario | Trigger | Expected error label | Plot behavior | Code behavior |
|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|
| Completion-stage error | malformed `expr` such as `~` | `Input error:` | plot clears / stays empty | code stays blank |
| Upload validation error | upload `bad_extension.txt` | `Input error:` | plot stays empty | code stays blank |
| Plot-stage error | missing object such as `unknown_object` | `Plot error:` | plot clears / stays empty | code remains visible |
| Recovery after error | correct the inputs and redraw | no error panel | valid plot renders | code updates normally |

## Setup

Assumptions:

-   Run this from the repo root.
-   Use the maintained package path centered on `R/paintr-app.R`, `R/paintr-parse.R`, `R/paintr-runtime.R`, `R/paintr-ui.R`, and `R/paintr-upload.R`.
-   Use the committed fixture files under `tests/testthat/fixtures/` for upload checks.

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggpaintr)
library(shiny)
library(shinyWidgets)
library(rlang)
library(assertthat)

csv_fixture <- file.path("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "simple_numeric.csv")
rds_fixture <- file.path("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "simple_numeric.rds")
bad_fixture <- file.path("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "bad_extension.txt")

sales_manual <- data.frame(
  day = as.Date("2024-01-01") + 0:5,
  value = c(10, 13, 12, 16, 18, 17),
  segment = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C", "C")
)

spaced_manual <- data.frame(left = 1:4, right = c(2, 4, 6, 8), check.names = FALSE)
names(spaced_manual) <- c("first column", "second column")
spaced_manual_clean <- ptr_normalize_column_names(spaced_manual)

date_placeholder <- ptr_define_placeholder(
  keyword = "date",
  build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {
    shiny::dateInput(id, copy$label)
  },
  resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {
    if (is.null(value) || identical(as.character(value), "")) {
      return(ptr_missing_expr())
    }

    rlang::expr(as.Date(!!as.character(value)))
  },
  copy_defaults = list(label = "Choose a date for {param}")
)

date_placeholders <- ptr_merge_placeholders(
  list(date = date_placeholder)
)

date_ui_text <- list(
  defaults = list(
    date = list(label = "Pick any date")
  ),
  params = list(
    xintercept = list(
      date = list(label = "Reference date")
    )
  ),
  layers = list(
    geom_vline = list(
      date = list(
        xintercept = list(
          help = "Choose a cutoff date for the vertical guide."
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

date_ui_text <- ptr_merge_ui_text(
  date_ui_text,
  placeholders = date_placeholders
)


manual_ids <- ptr_build_ids(
  control_panel = "builder_controls",
  draw_button = "render_plot",
  plot_output = "main_plot",
  error_output = "main_error",
  code_output = "main_code"
)

list(
  csv_fixture = csv_fixture,
  rds_fixture = rds_fixture,
  bad_fixture = bad_fixture
)

devtools::load_all(".")
```

Upload fixture paths to use during manual testing:

-   `r csv_fixture`
-   `r rds_fixture`
-   `r bad_fixture`

Recommended RStudio flow:

-   restart R if you have an older `ggpaintr` namespace loaded
-   run the setup chunk
-   launch one app chunk
-   perform the listed checks
-   close the app window before continuing

Use `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd` for known unresolved boundaries that
need repro code or follow-up testing rather than ordinary pass/fail workbook
checks.

Low-level developer note:

-   If you are manually probing `ptr_exec()`, discover its input ids
    with `ptr_runtime_input_spec()` instead of hand-authoring raw ids.
-   Raw ids such as `ggplot+3+2` may still appear in printed output, but they
    are implementation details rather than the recommended documentation path.



## Formula 13: Layer Switcher (picker + hidden tabset + sub-tabs)

This app exercises the new control-panel layout: a `pickerInput()` chooses the active layer, a hidden `tabsetPanel()` swaps panel bodies, and each layer's body splits into "Data" + "Controls" sub-tabs when both kinds of controls exist (or just "Data" / just flat controls when only one kind exists). The single "Update plot" button stays below the tabset.

```{r formula-13, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
mtcars |> head(num) |> ggplot(aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(size = num, alpha = num) +
  geom_smooth(method = text) +
  labs(title = text)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

-   open the **Layer** picker and confirm it lists `ggplot`, `geom_point`, `geom_smooth`, `labs`
-   pick `ggplot` and confirm two sub-tabs appear: **Data** + **Controls**
-   inside **Data**, set the `head(num)` input and click **Update data**
-   inside **Controls**, pick `x = mpg`, `y = disp`
-   pick `geom_point` and confirm only a flat block of controls (no sub-tabs); set `size = 2`, `alpha = 0.6`
-   pick `geom_smooth` and confirm only a flat block; set `method = "lm"` (no quotes typed in)
-   pick `labs` and set `title = Switcher demo`
-   click **Update plot**

Expected checks:

-   Selecting a different layer in the picker swaps the visible panel body without reloading the page.
-   Only one panel body is visible at a time; the others remain in the DOM but hidden (no horizontal tab strip).
-   The `ggplot` layer panel has two sub-tabs (`Data`, `Controls`) because it has both pipeline and non-pipeline placeholders.
-   The `geom_point`, `geom_smooth`, and `labs` panels render their controls flat (no inner tabset) because they have no data pipeline.
-   The `Update data` button only appears inside a Data sub-tab.
-   The `Update plot` button is rendered exactly once, sitting below the tabset.
-   The `ggplot` panel does NOT show an "Include this layer in the plot" checkbox; every other layer panel does.
-   Toggling each non-ggplot layer's checkbox visually grays out (`opacity ≈ 0.5`) the panel's content while keeping the inputs interactive; the layer is removed from the plot and from `outputCode` while unchecked.
-   Re-checking a previously-unchecked layer restores both the visual state and the layer in the plot/code.

## Formula 13B: Picker + Sub-Tab `ui_text` Overrides

```{r formula-13b, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app(
  "mtcars |> head(num) |> ggplot(aes(x = var, y = var)) +
     geom_point(size = num)",
  ui_text = list(
    shell = list(
      layer_picker = list(label = "Choose layer"),
      data_subtab = list(label = "Pipeline"),
      controls_subtab = list(label = "Aesthetics")
    )
  )
)
```

Expected checks:

-   The picker label reads `Choose layer` (not `Layer`).
-   The `ggplot` panel's sub-tabs are labeled `Pipeline` and `Aesthetics` (not `Data` / `Controls`).
-   Other defaults (`Update plot`, `Update data`, layer checkbox label) remain unchanged.

## Formula 14: Data-Pipeline Placeholders (single layer)

This app exercises the new data-pipeline workflow: placeholders inside a `data = ...` chain are tracked separately, edited inside the Data sub-tab, and applied only when the user clicks **Update data**. The cached data drives the `var` pickers and the rendered plot until the next click.

```{r formula-14, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
mtcars |> filter(cyl == num) |> head(num) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point()
")
```

Suggested manual inputs (in order):

1.  Open the `ggplot` layer's **Data** sub-tab.
2.  Confirm two `num` controls appear, labeled `Enter a number for filter()` and `Enter a number for head()` (verb-named, not generic).
3.  Set `filter(num) = 6`, `head(num) = 10`. Confirm a "stale data" hint or styling appears on the **Update data** button before clicking.
4.  Click **Update data**.
5.  Switch to the **Controls** sub-tab and pick `x = mpg`, `y = disp`. Confirm both pickers list mtcars columns (proving the cache resolved successfully).
6.  Click **Update plot**.
7.  Change `head(num)` to `3` but do NOT click **Update data** yet.
8.  Inspect the code panel and the plot.
9.  Click **Update data**, then **Update plot** again.

Expected checks:

-   `var` pickers are empty / disabled until the first **Update data** click successfully resolves the pipeline.
-   After **Update data** succeeds, both `x` and `y` pickers offer mtcars column names.
-   The plot renders only after **Update plot** is clicked.
-   `outputCode` shows the full piped expression with the values that were active at the last **Update data** click — not the current typed inputs.
-   When step 7 leaves an unapplied edit on the Data tab, a stale-data signal is visible (CSS class on the **Update data** button or accompanying notice). Clicking **Update data** clears the signal.
-   Generated code matches the cache, not the typed-but-unapplied input.
-   Selecting a column in `x` then re-clicking **Update data** preserves the previously-selected column name as long as it still exists in the refreshed dataset.

## Formula 14B: Multiple Data-Pipeline Layers

This formula puts a different pipeline on `ggplot()` and on `geom_point()`, exercising the per-layer independence of the Data sub-tab + Update Data button.

```{r formula-14b, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
mtcars |> head(num) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(data = mtcars |> dplyr::filter(num > 0), color = 'red')
")
```

Expected checks:

-   The `ggplot` layer panel and the `geom_point` layer panel each have their own **Data** sub-tab with their own placeholder controls.
-   Each layer has its own **Update data** button; clicking one does not retrigger the other.
-   `outputCode` reflects each layer's last applied pipeline independently.
-   Clicking **Update data** on one layer does not reset the typed-but-unapplied state on the other layer.

## Formula 14C: Nested `data = ...` Pipe Source Preservation

```{r formula-14c, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(
  data = mtcars |> filter(cyl == num) |> head(num),
  mapping = aes(x = var, y = var)
) + geom_point()
")
```

Expected checks:

-   Pipeline placeholder controls still appear inside the `ggplot` layer's Data sub-tab even though the chain lives inside a named `data = ...` argument.
-   `outputCode` after **Update data** + **Update plot** includes the `mtcars |> filter(...) |> head(...)` pipeline verbatim with the typed numeric values substituted.

## Formula 14D: Text-Placeholder Quote Stripping in a Pipeline

```{r formula-14d, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
iris |> dplyr::filter(Species != text) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point()
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

-   In the Data sub-tab, type `"setosa"` (with quotes) into the `text` control.
-   Click **Update data**, then **Update plot**.

Expected checks:

-   The control hints that quotes are not required (e.g. tooltip / placeholder text "no quotes").
-   The cached data and the generated code use a single quoted string, not a double-quoted one (`Species != "setosa"`, not `Species != "\"setosa\""`).
-   Removing the quotes from the input and clicking **Update data** + **Update plot** produces the same result.

## Final Checklist

-   The manual sweep covers wrapper apps, upload flows, copy rules,
    embedded/bind-helper integrations, custom plot rendering, and custom
    placeholders.
-   Plot updates after clicking `draw`.
-   `outputCode` matches the active inputs.
-   Blank optional `text`, `num`, and `expr` values disappear cleanly from generated code where expected.
-   Unchecked layers disappear from both the plot and `outputCode`.
-   Unchecking every optional layer still leaves a valid base `ggplot(...)`
    render instead of a brittle runtime failure.
-   Upload-backed `var` controls appear only after valid upload.
-   Bad-extension uploads show inline errors without crashing the session or
    affecting unrelated layers.
-   Default upload naming works when the dataset name box is blank.
-   Custom upload naming works when a dataset name is provided.
-   Combined global + layer-specific upload formulas maintain independent upload
    controls, independent `var` pickers, and independent error paths.
-   Inline errors appear for malformed `expr`, bad upload types, and missing objects.
-   The plot clears and does not stay stale after an error.
-   Generated code remains visible when plot construction fails after completion succeeds.
-   Recovery after an error clears the error panel and restores normal plotting.
-   Default copy-rule wording appears for shell text, upload prompts, numeric hints, and unnamed faceting prompts.
-   No default control label shows parser-style fallback text such as `argument 1`.
-   Custom `ui_text` override only the targeted prompts while untargeted controls stay on the defaults.
-   Embedded apps built from `ptr_server_state()` and the bind helpers still support custom outputs or observers around `ptr_state`.
-   Custom `renderPlot()` workflows can use `ptr_extract_plot()` and keep the rest of the default runtime behavior intact.
-   Custom placeholder controls render correctly, participate in code generation, and work alongside built-in placeholders.
-   The layer switcher renders one outer panel per layer behind a `pickerInput()`; only the picked panel is visible at a time.
-   Per-layer Data + Controls sub-tabs appear only when the layer has both pipeline and non-pipeline placeholders; pipeline-only layers show just a Data sub-tab; controls-only layers render flat.
-   The single Update Plot button remains below the tabset; the Update Data button is per-layer and lives inside the layer's Data sub-tab.
-   Toggling a layer's checkbox grays out (`opacity ~ 0.5`) the layer's content and removes it from the plot/code without locking inputs.
-   `ui_text` overrides for `layer_picker`, `data_subtab`, and `controls_subtab` change the picker label and sub-tab labels.
-   Data-pipeline placeholder edits stay un-applied until **Update data** is clicked; a stale-data signal appears on the button when typed input differs from the cache.
-   `outputCode` mirrors the cache as of the last **Update data** click (typed-but-unapplied edits are not reflected until the next click).
-   `var` pickers populate from the cache after **Update data**; previously-selected columns are preserved across cache rebuilds when still present.
-   Pipeline placeholders survive nested `data = ...` arguments and label themselves with the enclosing verb (e.g. `Enter a number for filter()`).
-   `text` placeholders inside a pipeline strip matching outer quotes and the control hints "no quotes".
-   Remaining internal-only or philosophy-boundary edge cases are reviewed in
    `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd`.
